title: What Do You Do When You Lose Gigantic Megalodon Shark Vertebrae?
gist: Megalodon vertebrae unearthed in the 1970s and long missing from museum archives have resurfaced; a new Palaeontologia Electronica analysis using micro-CT confirms the shark's ~80-foot size, ages the specimen at 64-96 years, and documents the first-ever megalodon stomach contents (basking shark remains).

insights.claim: Megalodon vertebrae unearthed in the 1970s that disappeared from museum archives shortly after have resurfaced, and a new Palaeontologia Electronica analysis empirically confirms the maximum vertebral diameter of 23 centimeters.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Recovered specimens enable modern re-analysis of long-debated size estimates; basic paleontology with no market read.
insights.quote: they empirically confirm the maximum vertebral diameter of 23 centimeters reported in the literature
insights.locator: The rediscovery of the vertebrae was a true delight

insights.claim: Megalodon, the Miocene-era apex predator, measured nearly 80 feet long and weighed more than 90 tons, dining on prey up to massive whales.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Quantifies the scale of the largest known shark; context only.
insights.quote: Clocking in at nearly 80 feet long and weighing more than 90 tons, megalodon was the apex predator of the era
insights.locator: The largest shark to ever terrorize the oceans

insights.claim: Using micro-CT imaging to measure tree-ring-like growth bands, researchers determined the megalodon specimen was at least 64 years old at death and could have lived as long as 96 years.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Demonstrates non-destructive imaging extracting life-history data from fossils.
insights.quote: the megalodon specimen was at least 64 years old when it died and could have lived as long as 96 years
insights.locator: Using micro-CT imaging, they measured the growth bands

insights.claim: Scales and gill structures from smaller basking sharks found alongside the vertebrae were interpreted as the megalodon's stomach contents — the first documentation of stomach contents in the megalodon fossil record.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: A genuine first-of-kind dietary record; notable within paleontology.
insights.quote: This led us to interpret the basking shark elements to represent the stomach contents of the megalodon, which is the first documentation for the megalodon fossil record
insights.locator: A close examination of the rocks discovered alongside

insights.claim: Because sharks have cartilaginous skeletons that preserve poorly, there are no complete megalodon skeletons, making these relatively well-preserved vertebrae precious scientific commodities.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Explains why each recovered specimen is disproportionately valuable to the field.
insights.quote: these relatively well-preserved vertebrae are precious scientific commodities
